title: 🎙️ Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life
description: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: wildfire detection tech and BC Wildfire monitoring systems, satellite/drone surveillance for Chilcotin wilderness, wildlife tracking and conservation technology, watershed sensors and salmon habitat monitoring, weather stations and climate tracking in backcountry, avalanche forecasting tech, trail mapping and GPS systems, outdoor recreation apps and connectivity, backcountry communication devices (satellite messengers, Meshtastic), flood/drought prediction systems, remote cameras for wildlife research, and how technology helps manage and protect Cariboo wild spaces. Prefer articles that bridge wilderness/conservation AND technology. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] ‘Agriculture of life’: the Rio families growing bananas to protect the world’s largest urban forest
items.content_html:
In the middle of the city, traditional growers blend crops with native species to preserve Pedra Branca state park’s biodiversity

The sound of the scythes wielded by brothers Jorge and Ubirajara Cardia breaks the silence in the hills of Vargem Grande, in the south-west zone of Rio de Janeiro city. Quilombola from the Cafundá Astrogilda community, they harvest bananas the same way their ancestors used to. Every week, they select the bunches of prata, maçã, and Cavendish bananas, cut them down and, on the back of their mules, go down the hillside with the newly harvested crop.

Through sloping ways in the forest, they travel about 5km (3 miles) along paths first opened by the Indigenous Tupinambá people and enslaved workers of African descent.

Continue reading...

items.title: [Boing Boing] All five DNA/RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples
items.content_html:
Every nucleobase needed to build DNA and RNA — adenine, guanine, cytosine, thymine, and uracil — has been found in rock samples from the asteroid Ryugu, according to a study published in Nature Astronomy. Previous research in 2023 had found only uracil. — Read the rest

The post All five DNA/RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Wander: a decentralized console for the small web
items.content_html:
A new project called Wander lets you drop two files onto your personal website — an index.html and a wander.js — and turn it into a node in a decentralized web console. Visitors click a button and are sent to a random page on your site, then to a site you've linked to, then to a site that one links to, going one hop deeper into the network with each click. — Read the rest

The post Wander: a decentralized console for the small web appeared first on Boing Boing.
